methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $32M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $1.8B.

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: The Bot Company
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Monumental is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Salar al Khafaji and Sebastiaan Visser, having raised $32M to date. The peer universe comprises 9 private companies and 1 public comparable across robotics and manufacturing, including Series B and C-stage firms such as Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Hadrian ($1.6B), and Nimble Robotics ($1.0B).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $32M total raised as a baseline proxy and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $61M. Peer-median analysis derives valuation from four same-sector and same/adjacent-stage comparables, producing a median of $1.6B with a range spanning $1.0B to $1.8B.

The triangulation of both methods supports a valuation range of $52M (low) to $1.9B (high), with a median of $843M. The wide spread reflects material tension between stage-based progression and peer market valuations in this sector. Key uncertainties include whether Monumental's operational metrics and market traction align with the higher-valuation peers, and the degree to which recent robotics funding dynamics have compressed or expanded valuations relative to the peer sample. Additional clarity on revenue, unit economics, and deployment pace would narrow this range materially.
